title: Stored XSS via Details panel — unescaped comment_author rendered as HTML in admin
affected_versions: all known (logic present in current codebase)

root_cause.server_issue: Returns raw unsanitized comment_author values from database via wp_send_json_success()
root_cause.client_issue: Renders server response items via jQuery .html() using string concatenation with no HTML encoding

taint_flow.result: Malicious comment stored in wp_comments with comment_approved='0'

taint_flow.action: Navigates to Tools > Sweep, clicks 'Details' button for 'Unapproved Comments' row
ajax_sweep_details() verifies nonce and capability, calls details('unapproved_comments')
taint_flow.result: SELECT comment_author FROM comments WHERE comment_approved='0' returns malicious payload

taint_flow.action: wp_send_json_success($details) returns raw payload without escaping

JS iterates data.data and does: html += '
  • ' + n + '
    • '; then .html(html)
taint_flow.result: Script tag injected into DOM and executed in admin context

also_affected_sweep_names: orphan_postmeta (meta_key — typically not user-controlled but depends on plugins)
impact: JavaScript execution in WordPress admin session. Attacker can: steal admin cookies/session tokens, create new admin accounts via REST API, install or modify plugins/themes, exfiltrate site data, or pivot to server if WP admin has file edit capabilities.
exploitation_difficulty: easy — comment submission requires no authentication, and modern WordPress sites routinely receive spam comments

false_positive_analysis.nonce_check: Does NOT prevent exploitation — nonce is valid (generated for legitimate admin session, used in their own browser)
false_positive_analysis.capability_check: Does NOT prevent exploitation — the triggering admin legitimately has the capability; the attacker exploits the rendering of their stored data
false_positive_analysis.comment_moderation: Comments with comment_approved='0' are specifically shown in the Unapproved Comments sweep — admins reviewing this are likely candidates to click Details
false_positive_analysis.wp_sanitization: WordPress sanitizes comment_author on front-end display with esc_html() but this AJAX endpoint bypasses that and reads raw DB values

remediation.preferred_fix: Server-side: apply esc_html() to each value in WPSweep::details() before returning, or wrap in wp_send_json_success(array_map('esc_html', $details)). This is the most robust fix.
remediation.alternative_fix:
Client-side: in js/wp-sweep.js, replace string concatenation with DOM text node creation: replace `html += '
  • ' + n + '
    • '` with DOM manipulation using .text() to set content, preventing HTML interpretation.
remediation.defense_in_depth: Both server and client fixes should be applied. Server-side escaping ensures safety regardless of how output is consumed. Client-side encoding provides defense-in-depth.
remediation.server_code_fix: In WPSweep::details(), before the return statement: return apply_filters('wp_sweep_details', array_map('esc_html', $details), $name);

references: wp_kses_post() and esc_html() for output escaping in WordPress
